title: Souls and Cores
wikitext:
[]: Souls and Cores             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Dec 31, 2011 3:48 pm Reichu tells me this hasn't been discussed in depth, and there's probably a good reason for that. But I'll throw it out there anyway to see what people think. How exactly do you suppose souls are bound to the cores of the Angels/Evas? Is the core something akin to a jar, such that breaking it releases the soul? Or is it bound more tightly, meaning the fate of the soul is tied to the fate of the core itself (meaning a destroyed core likewise destroys the soul bound to it)? I think the first description is the default assumption, and I tend to favor it myself, but it seems problematic for a number of reasons: First, it makes Yui's and Gendo's plans somewhat sketchy at best. If the core is a jar Gendo had everything he needed to bring Yui back: He had her soul in Unit 01's core, he had a means of destroying said core (and, once Rei successfully synced with Unit 00, a foolproof means of covering it up), a means of harvesting Yui's soul and depositing it in a new body, and a cover identity to keep her safe from the prying eyes of SEELE (they wouldn't be able to tell she wasn't Rei, after all). Further, this approach would also render Unit 01 useless as a medium for Third Impact, which means Gendo could stop SEELE in their tracks once he finds a convenient excuse to discard the Lance (something he could manage as early as Sahaquiel). Obviously, this would result in a very different show, but given Gendo's goals it seems to be a no- brainer. Second, in EoE we see Asuka connect with Kyoko in Unit 02, but after she's killed we never see her again; she doesn't show up in Asuka's Instrumentality sequences at all. I ordinary wouldn't think it noteworthy, but since she just showed up it seems to be the sort of thing that would be addressed. Third, Yui didn't get out of Unit 01 after 3I, despite the fact that GNR (or, if nothing else, the Lance) could easily have destroyed Unit 01 and freed her soul. Leaving aside the fact that she might have been batshit insane by that point I think it's reasonable to assume she would have bailed on Eva if it could have been safely destroyed and her soul released. I'm sure we can handwave all of these things if we work at it, but it seems to me the simplest approach is to assume that breaking the core doesn't release the soul; it's housed within the core, certainly, but the soul is bound to the Eva/Angel tightly enough that the core's destruction doesn't spell freedom for its inhabitant. Why? I dunno. But the niggling questions above leave me thinking this has to be the case, inelegant as it might seem (and even this doesn't work for the third question, as we see in NGE2 that, at the very least, a SoL can yank a soul out of a destroyed core, which is presumably how Kaworu rounded up all the Evas and Angels and headed off to parts unknown. Maybe Lilith couldn't do that for Yui for some reason, what with the whole "I'm dying here" thing and all). Thoughts? I realize I'm overthinking this, but I'm wondering if there's something I'm missing.

[]: Re: Souls and Cores             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Sat Dec 31, 2011 4:00 pm Bagheera wrote: Reichu tells me this hasn't been discussed in depth, and there's probably a good reason for that. But I'll throw it out there anyway to see what people think. How exactly do you suppose souls are bound to the cores of the Angels/Evas? Is the core something akin to a jar, such that breaking it releases the soul? Or is it bound more tightly, meaning the fate of the soul is tied to the fate of the core itself (meaning a destroyed core likewise destroys the soul bound to it)? I think the first description is the default assumption, and I tend to favor it myself, but it seems problematic for a number of reasons:

[]: Re: Souls and Cores             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Dec 31, 2011 5:42 pm Bagheera wrote: Yui didn't get out of Unit 01 after 3I

[]: Re: Souls and Cores             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Dec 31, 2011 5:48 pm Reichu wrote: She'd decided all the way back in 2004 that she was going to be staying in Eva-01 for a very long time.

[]: Re: Souls and Cores             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Dec 31, 2011 6:02 pm Sneak-updated my post. Bagheera wrote: She seemed to assume that this was a one-way trip, and that nothing could bring her out of the core once she went in.

[]: Re: Souls and Cores             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Dec 31, 2011 6:16 pm Reichu wrote: Her chat with Fuyutsuki, in its entirety, indicates that Eva's purpose has something to do with living on after humanity dies out. This is eventually going to happen, no matter what. Both of them seem to think it's serious business... however the hell their conversation went from where it was in 21' to the end of the solar system and Eva surviving in space... (The available draft for EoE provides some possible hints.)

[]: Re: Souls and Cores             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Dec 31, 2011 6:42 pm Hey, remember what you said about replying to things before you've thought things through? Bagheera wrote: Wait, what? What happened to "get an AATF around it, even if just for a moment!"? That seemed unambiguous to me, though I never saw the moon runes myself.

[]: Re: Souls and Cores             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Dec 31, 2011 6:56 pm Bagheera wrote: It doesn't seem the sort of thing a typical Japanese woman with a loving family would pursue for its own end.

[]: Re: Souls and Cores             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Sun Jan 01, 2012 7:30 am Reichu wrote: And I don't think any handwaving is necessary. The Angels are freed from their cores as they die, hang out for a few months (or days, even), and get swept into the big helical vortex when everyone else does. You could look at it this way... HIP Preparation Stage #1: Free Angel souls by destroying their cores HIP Preparation Stage #2: Free Lilin souls by generating huge AATF HIP: Gather all human souls together and make perfection

[]: Re: Souls and Cores             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Jan 01, 2012 1:18 pm Reichu wrote: Maybe symbv can provide further enlightenment.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Jan 01, 2012 4:49 pm Eh, I think she knew what they were generally on about long before Fuyu and Gendo were even in the picture, and this whole thing was her playing the long game on everyone to get to the end of the human 'choose your own adventure' story that she desired.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Jan 01, 2012 4:56 pm NemZ wrote: Eh, I think she knew what they were generally on about long before Fuyu and Gendo were even in the picture, and this whole thing was her playing the long game on everyone to get to the end of the human 'choose your own adventure' story that she desired.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jan 01, 2012 6:42 pm So, essentially, you think the only reason Yui committed to leaving Earth was because she was justifying the fact that there was no way she could leave the Eva without destroying her soul, or something? (It's kind of hard to follow all of this, and how it ultimately relates to keeping Yui and Gendo from being "stupid".) Sent a summons for symbv. as we see in NGE2 that, at the very least, a SoL can yank a soul out of a destroyed core, which is presumably how Kaworu rounded up all the Evas and Angels and headed off to parts unknown

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Jan 01, 2012 7:04 pm Reichu wrote: So, essentially, you think the only reason Yui committed to leaving Earth was because she was justifying the fact that there was no way she could leave the Eva without destroying her soul, or something?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jan 01, 2012 7:17 pm Bagheera wrote: More I think she assumed she couldn't leave, period, and made the best of it

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Jan 01, 2012 9:38 pm Reichu wrote: The show seems to tell us that the only reason the salvage operation failed was because Yui didn't want to come out. (Confer with episode 20 in general.) So she could have left -- but she had reasons for not doing so.

[]: Re: Souls and Cores             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Jan 01, 2012 10:21 pm Bagheera wrote: Second, in EoE we see Asuka connect with Kyoko in Unit 02, but after she's killed we never see her again; she doesn't show up in Asuka's Instrumentality sequences at all. I ordinary wouldn't think it noteworthy, but since she just showed up it seems to be the sort of thing that would be addressed.

[]: Re: Souls and Cores             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Jan 01, 2012 10:26 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Kyoko's around in the NGE2-PSP version of Asuka's (EoTV and EoE hybrid) Instrumentality Good End, for what it's worth.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jan 01, 2012 11:23 pm Bagheera wrote: I'm not sure it's that cute and dried.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Jan 01, 2012 11:28 pm Reichu wrote: "Cute and--"?
